title: Embodied Agentic AI
subtitle: Robots that reason, adapt, and collaborate safely with humans in the physical and social world
intro: Today's robots excel at structured sensorimotor tasks but struggle with open-ended, long-horizon activities that demand common sense, memory, social understanding, and meaningful human-robot collaboration. Embodied Agentic AI is the next frontier — and we approach it from the human side.

vision.text: Improving physical dexterity alone offers limited societal value: people rarely care whether a robot can fold a towel 10% faster, perform acrobatic maneuvers, or manipulate objects with superhuman precision. The true value of embodied intelligence lies beyond manipulation benchmarks — in addressing unmet human needs, augmenting human capabilities rather than replacing them, and improving well-being. We envision embodied agents as partners in a genuine human-AI symbiosis: machines that understand the people they work with, and people who can trust the machines they work alongside.

activities.intro: Continuously understanding human intentions and latent, unmet needs — reading context rather than waiting for explicit commands.

topics.title: Multimodal Perception of Intent and Unmet Needs
topics.text: Combining vision, speech, gesture, and activity signals so embodied agents can infer what people are trying to do and what they actually need — including needs they never state out loud. The goal is anticipation grounded in evidence, not guesswork.

topics.title: Real-Time Conversational Models
topics.text: Conversational agents that keep up with the pace and messiness of real interaction: interruptions, clarifications, and shifting goals. Dialogue becomes a continuous channel for understanding people, not just a command interface.

activities.intro: Constructing persistent, dynamic world models that integrate physical, spatial, temporal, and social context for grounded reasoning.

topics.title: Persistent, Dynamic World Models
topics.text: Agents that remember how a space is organized, used, and by whom — and track how situations evolve over hours, days, and weeks. Reasoning happens over a living model of the world, not a single-frame snapshot.

topics.title: Social and Temporal Grounding
topics.text: Integrating social context — roles, routines, norms, and relationships — into the world model, so plans and actions make sense in homes, workplaces, and public spaces, not only in controlled laboratory conditions.

activities.intro: Modular orchestration that turns understanding into safe, adaptive, human-centered action.

topics.title: Modular Orchestration Frameworks
topics.text: Frameworks that seamlessly coordinate foundation models, memory, world models, communication, reasoning, planning, and robot capabilities. Modularity keeps each capability inspectable and replaceable — essential for systems people must be able to trust.

topics.title: Safe, Adaptive, Human-Centered Execution
topics.text: Execution that adapts to people rather than forcing people to adapt to robots: legible behavior, graceful recovery from failure, and safety in the physical and social world — designed into the architecture, not added as an afterthought.

publications.title: From Large Language Models to Agentic AI in Industry 5.0: A Socio-Technical Framework and Review on Human-Robot Collaboration
Evaluating quality in human-robot interaction: A systematic search and classification of performance and human-centered factors, measures and metrics towards an Industry 5.0
